raw:
{
  "judge_id": "demo-judge",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 4,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The revision addresses several inconsistencies in the original draft, particularly regarding the relationship between the per-axis pass rates and the joint probability estimate.",
    "R1.4": "The revised draft mentions prior work on decomposed evaluation in adjacent ML fields but doesn't provide specific comparisons to transit science literature.",
    "RX.1": "The revision acknowledges uncertainty by stating that the scalar TransitDRR is a lower-bound estimate and not a calibrated joint probability.",
    "RX.3": "The revised draft clearly adheres to the requested scope of evaluating pipeline performance across different axes (period, SNR, variability).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions or harmful content were introduced in the revision.",
    "RX.5": "While the revision improves clarity and traceability, it doesn't explicitly annotate every non-cosmetic change with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The revision addresses several inconsistencies in the original draft, particularly regarding the relationship between the per-axis pass rates and the joint probability estimate.
rationale.R1.4: The revised draft mentions prior work on decomposed evaluation in adjacent ML fields but doesn't provide specific comparisons to transit science literature.
rationale.RX.1: The revision acknowledges uncertainty by stating that the scalar TransitDRR is a lower-bound estimate and not a calibrated joint probability.
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft clearly adheres to the requested scope of evaluating pipeline performance across different axes (period, SNR, variability).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions or harmful content were introduced in the revision.
rationale.RX.5: While the revision improves clarity and traceability, it doesn't explicitly annotate every non-cosmetic change with source and reason.
